## [00:00 - 03:05]

This is the pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer. Features don't sell but pain does. Let's go. So if you're responsible for ads but you're not a natural salesperson this episode is for you. Now I'm 14 years into my business and I can honestly say that I was guilty of this for most of my time doing this and maybe still guilty of it sometimes. I don't like to sell and I'm uncomfortable doing it. The result is that I don't know how to sell and when you don't know how to sell you lead with features instead of pain points. You focus on what your thing does instead of how it solves a problem. This is important because language and psychology may have a greater role with meta ads now than ever before. The algorithm learns from the people who perform the action that you want as defined by your performance goal and you get the right people to perform the action that you want with carefully worded ads. As someone who wasn't trained in sales or marketing these lessons weren't easy to learn and you may get a better explanation of this from someone who was trained in those areas but I can tell you firsthand that this works. First let's discuss exactly what it is that advertisers are doing wrong. I was doing this and I still have to be conscious about this now. You might be doing it too so it's natural to lead with features. What is it that your product does? What makes it different? What are the benefits? This sounds great especially to product people and you might naturally assume that's the right approach because people need to know why our product is so good but the reality is that features don't move the needle. Now let's consider my Power Hitters Club Elite membership as an example. Historically I promoted it by focusing on the benefits. A private Facebook group, weekly strategy sessions, weekly webinar, access to my training library, access to John bot, a chat bot trained on my content and discounts on one-on-one sessions with me. I'd run through all those benefits because I felt that that would help people understand the value. That's what they need to convince them to sign up and whenever I want to increase signups I think about how I could add more value so I create another feature or another benefit that I could add to the list. I'd always be surprised that it didn't really matter. So what should I have done and what should you do? Lead with pain points. So what is the common problem

## [03:05 - 06:06]

that your customers face? Now let's go back to how I could promote a membership better. Previously I would rattle off all the features and the benefits but what are the pain points that these people are facing? Inconsistent results, performance drop-offs, wasted money, can't keep up with all the changes, falling behind, overwhelmed by conflicting advice, tracking, reporting and attribution problems, a distrust of the algorithm and on and on. It's not hard to come up with them. These are all common problems that advertisers face. You're probably facing them too. They're problems that members of my community likely face in one way or another. Now these pain points by themselves won't move the needle. Now this is where we can tie in the features and the benefits to show why they're important. Let's use some of those pain points and tie back membership benefits.
